title: Hearty German Pork Stew
description: A classic German-inspired stew made with tender pork and robust flavors.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the pork cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the pork from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion, carrot, and celery to the pot and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the beer, beef broth, apple cider vinegar, thyme, salt, and pepper to the pot.
Return the pork to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the pork is tender.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
Serve with crusty bread or over egg noodles.
